title: Raita (Yogurt Condiment)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Thick Yogurt (Dahi) – 500 g (about 2 cups) full-fat, unsweetened, preferably strained for creaminess
Cucumber – 1 medium, peeled, deseeded, and finely diced or grated
Onion – ½ small, very finely minced (optional, adds crunch but use sparingly in fine dining)
Tomato – ½ medium, deseeded and finely diced (optional for color and freshness)
Fresh Coriander Leaves – 2 tbsp, finely chopped
Fresh Mint Leaves – 1 tbsp, finely chopped (or use ½ tsp dried mint powder)
Roasted Cumin Powder – 1 tsp (roast whole cumin seeds until aromatic, then grind)
Black Salt (Kala Namak) – ½ tsp (essential for the restaurant-style tang)
Green Chili – 1 small, deseeded and very finely minced (optional, for heat)
Chilled Water or Milk – 2–3 tbsp (only if you want a thinner consistency)

instruction.title: Method
steps: Prepare the Yogurt Base: Whisk the thick yogurt until smooth and lump-free. If you prefer a lighter consistency, whisk in 2–3 tbsp chilled water or milk.
Prepare the Vegetables: Peel and deseed cucumber (to avoid excess water), then finely dice or grate. Deseed and finely chop the tomato (if using). Finely mince onion and green chili.

Season the Raita: Add cucumber, tomato, onion, coriander, and mint to the yogurt. Mix in roasted cumin powder, black salt, and regular salt. Taste and adjust seasoning—restaurant chefs often balance black salt with a pinch of regular salt for depth.
Chill Before Serving: Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow flavors to meld. Stir once before serving; garnish with a pinch of cumin powder and a sprig of mint.

chefTips: For Extra Creaminess: Strain the yogurt through muslin cloth for 30 minutes before using.
Flavor Infusion: Lightly bruise mint leaves before chopping to release oils.
Presentation: Serve in a chilled bowl with a light dusting of cumin powder on top for visual appeal.
Pairing: Perfect with biryani, kebabs, or spicy curries to balance heat.
